formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Since the "Crisis of the Constitutional Order" chapter in CBSE Class 12 Political Science is a non-numerical subject, it won't have mathematical formulas. Instead, I will provide key statements and laws in the same format:

[
  {
    "name": "Basic Structure Doctrine",
    "formula": "Not applicable",
    "variables": "N/A",
    "units": "N/A",
    "notes": "A judicial doctrine that limits the power of the Parliament to amend the Constitution. It emphasizes the importance of protecting the 'basic structure' of the Constitution."
  },
  {
    "name": "Rule of Law",
    "formula": "Not applicable",
    "variables": "N/A",
    "units": "N/A",
    "notes": "A fundamental principle of governance that emphasizes the supremacy of law and the equality of all citizens before the law."
  },
  {
    "name": "Separation of Powers",
    "formula": "Not applicable",
    "variables": "N/A",
    "units": "N/A",
    "notes": "A constitutional principle that divides power among the legislative, executive, and judicial branches of government to prevent any one branch from becoming too powerful."
  },
  {
    "name": "Rule of Law and the Constitution",
    "formula": "Not applicable",
    "variables": "N/A",
    "units": "N/A",
    "notes": "A principle that the Constitution is the supreme law of the land and all citizens are equal before the law."
  }
]

Please note that there are no mathematical formulas in this chapter, and the above output is a representation of key statements and laws in the format requested.
